description:
## Background

The first Child Issues slice establishes the hierarchy section but deliberately preserves loaded order and always displays its empty state. Issue Detail should ultimately match Beadwork's child ordering convention and avoid spending detail space on a hierarchy section that has no content.

Triage: ready-for-agent

## What to build

Refine the existing Child Issues section so entries are ordered by priority from P0 through P4, then by creation date oldest first. When the selected Issue has no Child Issues, omit the entire Child Issues section rather than rendering its prior empty state.

## Technical Approach

- Build on the Child Issue derivation established by bsm-nd7.1.
- Sort a derived collection without mutating the explorer's loaded `allIssues` data.
- Use Beadwork-compatible ordering: ascending numeric priority, then ascending creation timestamp.
- Render the section conditionally only when the sorted Child Issue collection is non-empty.

## Considerations

- Keep derivation local to presentation state; do not add a durable Beadsmith hierarchy model or make a new CLI call.
- Preserve the required Child Issue entry content and section position after Dependencies.
- Define deterministic handling in tests for ties and dates as represented by the existing Issue contract.
- This task does not make entries navigable; that remains separate work.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Child Issues are ordered P0 through P4, then oldest to newest by creation date.
- [ ] Sorting does not reorder `allIssues` or the Issue List.
- [ ] Issue Detail omits the Child Issues heading and content when no Child Issues exist.
- [ ] Existing populated Child Issues rendering remains available after Dependencies.
- [ ] Focused tests cover priority ordering, creation-date tie breaking, and absent-section behavior.

## Test Plan

- Add/update pure derivation tests for ordering without input mutation.
- Extend Issue Explorer component tests for conditional visibility.
- Run the affected frontend test suite and Ultracite checks.

comments.text:
# Implementation Plan: bsm-nd7.2 — Polish Child Issue ordering and empty-state visibility

## Goal

Refine the Child Issues slice delivered by bsm-nd7.1 without changing its data source, detail placement, entry content, or read-only behavior:

- derive the selected Issue's Child Issues from the successful explorer state's `allIssues` collection;
- order them by numeric priority ascending (P0 through P4), then by creation timestamp oldest first;
- omit the complete Child Issues section when the selected Issue has no matching children.

The populated section remains immediately after Dependencies and before Other metadata. Each entry continues to show ID, humanized status, and title in that order. Child Issue navigation remains bsm-nd7.3.

## Prerequisite and existing seam

bsm-nd7.1 must land first. This plan assumes its implementation provides:

- `src/issues/issue-hierarchy.ts` with the pure Child Issue derivation seam;
- `IssueExplorer` derivation from successful `issueState.allIssues`, matched by exact `child.parent === selectedIssue.id`;
- a `childIssues` prop passed through `IssueDetailPane` to `IssueDetailContent`;
- a semantic, read-only Child Issues section rendered after Dependencies, including the prior empty state.

Do not reimplement that vertical slice or change the active Issue List View, search, or selected-Issue semantics while applying this task.

## Domain and repository constraints

- A Child Issue is an Issue whose Beadwork `parent` references the selected Issue. The relationship is derived from the Current Workspace's loaded Issue List; it is not a durable field on the parent.
- `Issue.priority` is numeric and `Issue.created` is a string in the existing RPC contract. Beadwork creates UTC RFC3339 timestamps, and its own list implementation sorts by priority and then `created` string ascending (`docs/research/infra/beadwork/internal/issue/list.go` and `issue.go`). Use that same comparison model rather than parsing human-readable `bw show` output or adding date/time policy in Beadsmith.
- `allIssues` must remain the source collection. No new `bw` command, RPC/Rust type, hierarchy model, or local persistence is needed.
- The issue list must retain its incoming order. Only the derived Child Issue collection may be sorted.
- Reuse `toIssueViewModel` and the existing status badge tone mapping for entry presentation; do not duplicate status-label logic.

## Dependency graph

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Use bsm-nd7.1 hierarchy seam] --> B[Add priority and creation ordering]
    B --> C[Update pure derivation tests]
    B --> D[Hide the empty detail section]
    C --> E[Run focused tests and checks]
    D --> E
```

## Implementation steps

### 1. Extend the pure Child Issue derivation module

Update `src/issues/issue-hierarchy.ts` rather than putting ordering logic in JSX.

- Keep exact `parent` matching and the `allIssues` input source established by bsm-nd7.1.
- Make the module's returned Child Issue collection the finalized presentation collection: first filter matching children, then sort the new derived array.
- Compare `priority` numerically in ascending order. Do not sort the rendered `P0`/`P1` labels lexicographically.
- For equal priorities, compare `created` ascending. Because Beadwork's created timestamps are UTC RFC3339 strings, lexicographic ordering matches chronological ordering and mirrors Beadwork's implementation.
- Sort only a fresh array (`filter` already creates one, or copy explicitly before sorting). Never call `sort` on `allIssues` or any array shared with Issue List View derivation.
- Return equal-priority/equal-timestamp entries in their incoming order through the runtime's stable sort behavior. Do not invent a third ID/title key that would diverge from the specified Beadwork-compatible keys.
- Keep the function pure: no RPC/CLI calls, mutation, durable hierarchy state, timestamp reads, or React dependencies.

A small private comparator is preferable to a second public sorting API unless the bsm-nd7.1 implementation already exposes a separate seam. The public interface should remain one focused function accepting the loaded Issue List and selected parent ID and returning a new ordered collection.

### 2. Update pure derivation coverage

Update `src/issues/issue-hierarchy.test.ts` using the existing Issue fixture conventions.

Cover the finalized contract:

- matching children are returned and unrelated Issues are excluded;
- children are ordered by priority first, proving a newer P0 precedes an older P4;
- children with the same priority are ordered oldest to newest by RFC3339 `created` value;
- two children with identical priority and `created` values retain their deterministic incoming order;
- exact parent matching remains unchanged, including exclusion of the selected parent and non-matching/whitespace-different parent IDs;
- no matches return an empty array;
- the original `allIssues` array remains byte-for-byte/order-for-order unchanged after derivation.

Update bsm-nd7.1's source-order assertions to reflect this new finalized ordering contract. Keep the test focused on IDs and ordering rather than snapshots or rendered markup.

### 3. Make Issue Detail visibility conditional

Update the bsm-nd7.1 changes in `src/issues/IssueExplorer.tsx`.

- Continue passing the ordered `childIssues` collection through the existing detail prop path.
- In `IssueDetailContent`, render the existing Child Issues section only when `childIssues.length > 0`.
- Keep the section directly after Dependencies and before Other metadata when populated.
- Preserve the existing semantic list markup and entry content/order: ID, status badge/humanized status, then title.
- Remove the old `No Child Issues` empty-state rendering for this slice. With zero children, render no Child Issues heading, section, list, or empty-state copy.
- Keep populated Child Issues read-only: no links, buttons, click handlers, navigation, or changes to keyboard/list selection behavior.
- Do not alter `deriveIssueExplorerState`, selected Issue lookup, list filtering, search behavior, loading/failure handling, or scroll behavior.

### 4. Extend Issue Explorer component coverage

Update `src/issues/IssueExplorer.test.tsx`.

- Change the detail section-flow expectation to retain `Child Issues` between `Dependencies` and `Other metadata` for a populated parent.
- Add a populated scenario with children intentionally supplied in an order different from the required priority/date order. Select the parent and assert that:
  - the Child Issues heading/section is present;
  - it remains immediately after Dependencies;
  - only children whose `parent` matches the selected parent appear;
  - the rendered entries follow priority/date order;
  - each entry contains ID, humanized status/badge, and title in the established order;
  - entries remain non-interactive (no new links or buttons).
- Add an absent-children scenario, select the Issue, and assert within the Issue Detail scope that the Child Issues heading and previous empty-state copy are both absent. This must not rely on the Issue List's unrelated content.
- Assert the Issue List's rendered row order remains the original loaded order after Child Issue sorting, guarding against an accidental in-place sort of `allIssues`.
- Preserve bsm-nd7.1 coverage proving children come from `allIssues` rather than the active Ready/Blocked collection where applicable; do not add navigation or cross-filter persistence assertions here.
- Use existing accessible role/section helpers and avoid CSS-dependent selectors.

## Validation

Run from the repository root after bsm-nd7.1 is available:

```bash
pnpm test -- src/issues/issue-hierarchy.test.ts src/issues/IssueExplorer.test.tsx
pnpm check
pnpm build
```

Review the final diff to confirm that changes are limited to the hierarchy derivation/tests and Issue Detail rendering/tests. In particular, there should be no changes to `src/rpc/bindings.ts`, Rust RPC code, Beadwork command invocation, Issue List View derivation, or Child Issue navigation/selection behavior.

## Acceptance checklist

- [ ] Child Issues are ordered by numeric priority ascending, then oldest `created` timestamp.
- [ ] Priority/date ordering is implemented in the pure hierarchy module, not in JSX.
- [ ] Sorting never mutates `allIssues` or changes Issue List order.
- [ ] A selected Issue with no Child Issues renders no Child Issues section, heading, list, or empty-state copy.
- [ ] A populated Child Issues section remains after Dependencies and preserves ID/status/title content order.
- [ ] Pure tests cover priority ordering, date ordering, exact ties, exclusion, no matches, and input immutability/order.
- [ ] Component tests cover populated ordering, placement, absent-section behavior, and unchanged Issue List order.
- [ ] Focused tests, Ultracite checks, and the TypeScript/Vite build pass.

## Non-goals and follow-up boundaries

- Do not add a `children` field to the Issue/RPC contract or a durable Beadsmith hierarchy model.
- Do not issue another `bw` command or parse `bw show` output.
- Do not change active Issue List View/search behavior or make Issue Detail selection All-Issues-backed.
- Do not make Child Issue entries navigable; that is bsm-nd7.3.
- Do not add real-desktop WebDriver coverage here; the completed Child Issues flow is proved by bsm-nd7.4.

title: Polish Child Issue ordering and empty-state visibility
